paper_title: Foraminiferal and environmental data analysis methods

methods.description: Procedure for preparing surface and core sediment samples and identifying benthic foraminifera for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: Both the surface and core samples were wet-sieved with a 63 µm sieve.
steps.searchable_summary: Wet sieve sediment samples using a 63 µm sieve.

steps.description: The fraction > 63 µm was dried at 40 • C for later foraminiferal analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry the sediment fraction greater than 63 µm at 40°C.

steps.description: The modern and fossil faunas were investigated from the > 125 µm fraction using representative splits containing approximately 300 benthic foraminiferal specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare representative splits of the > 125 µm fraction containing approximately 300 benthic foraminiferal specimens for investigation.

steps.description: The identification of the foraminifera on the species level was mainly based on the studies of Hottinger et al. (1993), Jones (1994) and Hohenegger et al. (1999).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify foraminifera specimens to species level using taxonomic guides.

steps.description: Rare species or specimens that could not be identified on the species level were grouped into their genus or family.
steps.searchable_summary: Group unidentified or rare foraminifera specimens at genus or family level.

steps.description: Potentially relocated benthic foraminifera (broken tests, tests fragments, and tests with yellowish-brown coloration) were counted separately.
steps.searchable_summary: Count potentially relocated foraminifera specimens separately based on specified criteria.

methods.description: Application of Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and Redundancy Analysis (RDA) to analyze foraminiferal assemblages and their relationship with environmental parameters.

steps.description: A total of 53 modern foraminiferal taxa having relative minimum abundances of 0.5 % on the total fauna and being present in at least 3 samples were included into analysis. All potentially relocated specimens were excluded.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter foraminiferal taxa data based on minimum relative abundance (0.5%), minimum sample presence (3), and exclude relocated specimens.
outputs: filtered foraminiferal taxa data for analysis

steps.description: In order to extract the dominant modern and fossil benthic foraminiferal assemblages, a principal component analysis (PCA) in Q-mode and with varimax rotation was carried out on the surface samples. The number of principal components (PCs) was selected based on eigenvalues > 1. PC loadings ≥ 0.4 for each axis were defined as significant.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Q-mode Principal Component Analysis (PCA) with varimax rotation on filtered foraminiferal data. Select components with eigenvalues > 1 and identify significant loadings (>= 0.4).
inputs: filtered foraminiferal taxa data for analysis

steps.description: We used a reduced surface data set with 23 species having percentages exceeding 5 % in the total assemblage. Species counts were square-root-transformed and environmental parameters were standardized before analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Create a reduced dataset with species exceeding 5% abundance. Square-root transform species counts and standardize environmental parameters.
outputs: reduced and transformed/standardized data for RDA

steps.description: In order to examine the relationship between the modern species in the data set of the surface samples and environmental parameters, a redundancy analysis (RDA) was carried out using Canoco (version 4.5).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Redundancy Analysis (RDA) to examine the relationship between foraminiferal species and environmental parameters.
inputs: reduced and transformed/standardized data for RDA

steps.description: Partial RDAs (with each environmental variable as the only variable) were calculated to evaluate the individual influence of the environmental parameters on the foraminiferal assemblages.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Partial Redundancy Analysis (RDA) for each environmental variable individually to assess its influence.
inputs: reduced and transformed/standardized data for RDA
